summary: Thunder‑charged Looks Like Rain and classic Scarlet>Fire sequence
blurb: The show delivers a thunder‑charged Looks Like Rain and an inspired Scarlet Begonias → Fire On the Mountain → Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of the World suite. Jerry’s guitar is on fire while Brent provides lush keyboards throughout. A solid first set rounds out the performance.
review:
May 15, 1981 at Rutgers University's Athletic Center captures the Dead in top form. The first set crackles with energy—Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo rolls into a seamless Franklin's Tower, and Candyman leads into a bluesy Little Red Rooster before the crowd is swept up by Ramble On Rose and a thunder‑charged Looks Like Rain that had the house howling. 

The second set is the highlight: a flawless Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain → Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World sequence that showcases Jerry’s powerful, soaring guitar tone, Brent’s rich, fully integrated keyboards, and Bob’s impassioned “don’t give up the fight” line during the Prophet. The rhythm section stays tight throughout, closing with a joyous U.S. Blues encore.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All four recordings are positive; reviewers consistently praise the thunder‑charged Looks Like Rain, the classic Scarlet>Fire>Estimated>Eyes sequence, and strong band playing. No negative comments appear across any analysis.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crystal‑clear audio, minimal technical flaws, and full detail of the band’s performance.

key_highlights: Thunder‑charged Looks Like Rain that electrifies the crowd.
Classic Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain > Estimated Prophet > Eyes Of The World suite.
Strong first‑set energy featuring Candyman, Little Red Rooster and Ramble On Rose.

band_performance.Jerry: Powerful guitar tone, energetic playing and strong vocals throughout, especially on Looks Like Rain and the Scarlet/Fire sequence.
band_performance.Bob: "Don't give up the fight" line during Estimated Prophet and solid vocal work on Looks Like Rain.
band_performance.Brent: Best all‑around keyboard player of the era; fully integrated, delivering tasty figures in Eyes Of The World and supporting the band’s dynamics.
